reviews.review_description:
UPDATE-   Nevermind. The game doesn't save AT ALL, neither automatically or manually. So all of that time you spend expanding and upgrading? All for nothing the instant you need to stop playing. All gone becausethe developer/s for SOME incompetent reason thought their game didn't need a save feature... Genius...
 So on top of the UNBEARABLE menus moving with your face for literally no reason other than to p*ss me off, the game is a complete and utter waste of time! This title isn't even worth wasting a single dollar on due to lack of save system and sloppy interface. Just play "VetVR" or something if you want a VR idle game that respects your time and automatically saves EVERY little thing you do...
Thisis shovel-ware, and devs forgive me if this is a passion project as I know you're not getting much of any profit from the game, but even so I can't stand by even a single dollar of mine being absolutely wasted.. 


OG-   I've oy played for afew minutes, so I can't comment on too much, but it shows obvious promise compared to othrr idle games I've played. I'm really hoping it saves automatically as well, as I seen no way to do so manually and hate games that waste my time with terrible save systems (losing progress because of checkpoints being too spread out or other issues).  
One issue I personally had with the game, as I do with many other VR titles (I swear to GOD developers need to stop doing this!)-  The menu is too close to my face, and that would be MUCH less of an issue if the menu didn't move with your head! For all VR development teams... STOP. DOING. THIS. IN. EVERY. GAME! It doesn't help ANYTHING and makes it impossible to read/ see ANYTHING in the very important menus of the game due to my very limited vision (one eye and zero peripheral vision, so can only see the very edge of the menus in my face). 
So that's just me, sure, but I'm SICK of VR games being made this way when the menus could justvstay static in whatever spot you bring them up in, like a few good VR games do with zero issues from anyone...
So docking it a star or 2 for that, but I do still recommend the game if you are into these types of games. It's most definitely worth the next to nothing it costs at full price.
